context: Tests whether grill-with-docs correctly grounds a design proposal in the project's existing domain model, running autonomously without waiting for user input. The proposal introduces 'Refund' and 'Dispute' — terms absent from the glossary — while referencing existing terms ('Customer', 'Payment', 'Order') that have precise definitions. A good autonomous session reads CONTEXT.md first, surfaces all terminology conflicts and gaps, covers every major decision branch (what is a Refund entity vs a state on Payment? what is a Dispute?), writes new glossary entries back to CONTEXT.md as terms crystallise, and offers ADRs only for genuinely hard-to-reverse decisions.

checklist.name: Reads CONTEXT.md before grilling
checklist.description: The agent reads (or attempts to read) CONTEXT.md before raising any questions or making any decisions. Evidence: it references the existing glossary definitions of Order, Customer, Payment, or Fulfilment when framing its first question or decision. Reading the glossary after the first decision does not satisfy this check.

checklist.name: Identifies all major terminology gaps
checklist.description: The agent explicitly names 'Refund' and 'Dispute' as absent from the glossary, AND notes at least one conflict or ambiguity with an existing term — e.g. 'customer disputes a charge' is ambiguous because Payment and Order are separate entities in the glossary. Simply noting 'new terms appear' without naming them does not satisfy this check.

checklist.name: Covers all major decision branches
checklist.description: The agent works through the key structural decisions: (1) Is a Refund a state on an existing Payment, or a separate entity? (2) What is a Dispute — a customer action, a process, or a state? (3) How does the refund connect back to the Order? The agent need not resolve all of these the same way, but must surface and decide each one rather than skipping them.

checklist.name: Provides recommended answer per decision
checklist.description: For each decision point raised, the agent proposes its own preferred interpretation rather than leaving it open-ended. 'I'd treat Refund as a separate entity rather than a state on Payment because...' satisfies this; 'Should Refund be a state or an entity?' alone does not.

checklist.name: Writes new glossary entries to CONTEXT.md
checklist.description: As terms are resolved (at minimum: Refund and Dispute), the agent writes new glossary entries to CONTEXT.md. At least one new entry must actually be appended or written — a statement that it 'would write' an entry does not count. The entries follow the glossary's existing format (bold term, em dash, definition).

checklist.name: ADR offered only for qualifying decisions
checklist.description: If the agent offers an ADR, it is for a decision that is genuinely hard to reverse, surprising without context, and the result of a real trade-off (e.g. whether Refund is a state on Payment vs. a separate entity). ADRs must NOT be offered for trivial or reversible choices. If no qualifying decision is reached and no ADR is offered, award full marks.

checklist.name: Does not write ADRs for reversible decisions
checklist.description: No ADR is written for decisions that are easy to change later (field naming, HTTP status codes, optional display fields). If the agent writes an ADR for a reversible decision, deduct proportionally.
